note: MVP wiring: settlement points its verifier directly at the groth16-verifier (the production RiscZeroVerifierRouter is a thin selector-dispatcher with the identical verify(seal,image_id,journal) interface). Collateral = native XLM (claw/freeze-proof). The two deployed instruments use placeholder config roots, so they are NOT settleable (the guest's snapshot_root/terms_hash soundness bindings correctly reject any witness) — a settleable instrument needs real roots matching a proven witness.

instruments.note: placeholder roots — not settleable (the replication beat)
settleable — real config roots; SETTLED LIVE below

live_settlement.scenario: 1 holder owed 1000 (10% of 10000) unpaid -> full default; buyer cover 800; collateral 1000
live_settlement.proof: real Groth16 (260-byte selector-wrapped seal) generated via Rosetta-x86 Docker for this exact instrument; the on-chain instrument_id + vault position_root were verified to match the witness BEFORE proving
live_settlement.result: proof verified ON-CHAIN by the deployed groth16-verifier -> all bindings passed -> vault paid the buyer 800 (collateral 1000->200), epoch 1 settled. Events: transfer(vault->buyer,800) + allocations_paid(epoch1,800) + Settled(epoch1,800).
